nodes.label: AI as learning-oriented tutor and rubber duck
nodes.label_negative: AI as code generator bypassing learning
nodes.grounding_span: I use it as a sort of hybrid 'rubber duck' and tutor

nodes.label: AI as research assistant for hard-to-find information
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable source requiring verification
nodes.grounding_span: I'll often use it for trying locate information that's tricky for me to search for

nodes.label: AI reliability and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: the models are all still far too hit and miss for me to blindly trust any of it

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner requiring human oversight
nodes.label_negative: AI as autonomous agent with blind acceptance
nodes.grounding_span: I'd say it's only ever collaborative

nodes.label: AI use in authentic creative work (reviews)
nodes.grounding_span: I'd never get it to generate any of the actual content for a review

nodes.label: AI as skill development enabler
nodes.grounding_span: I almost never use it to generate code, because that's not helpful for learning

nodes.label: Learning and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: there's a lot of learning going on

nodes.label: Authenticity and integrity in work
nodes.grounding_span: I am being paid to write that review, so I'm not going to farm that work out to an LLM

nodes.label: Efficiency and productivity
nodes.grounding_span: I'm on something of a deadline, I might get it to do some of the donkey work

nodes.label: Reliability and trustworthiness
nodes.grounding_span: the models are all still far too hit and miss for me to blindly trust any of it

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as learning aid
nodes.grounding_span: Being able to get answers to really specific questions... is really handy

nodes.label: Frustration with AI unreliability and outdated info
nodes.grounding_span: I've definitely wasted time going down rabbit-holes that were completely wrong

nodes.label: Resistance to AI in authentic creative tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I'd never get it to generate any of the actual content for a review

nodes.label: Cautious optimism about future AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I will at some point switch to using coding agents more, I'm pretty sure of that

nodes.label: Systematic verification and double-checking
nodes.grounding_span: I always have web-search enabled... my starting point is to look at the links it's pulled in

nodes.label: Learning-first approach with clear boundaries
nodes.grounding_span: I almost never use it to generate code, because that's not helpful for learning
